  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Hot work being performed directly over an open sewer/drain without isolation or gas monitoring",
    "No evidence of a valid hot work permit or atmospheric testing in progress",
    "Imminent fire and explosion hazard from flammable sewer gases"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop all hot work immediately",
    "Evacuate the area and barricade the hot work zone",
    "Purge, isolate, or cover the sewer/drain opening with a non-combustible, gas-tight barrier",
    "Conduct continuous atmospheric monitoring for flammable gases and vapors before and during any hot work",
    "Obtain a valid hot work permit after hazards are controlled"
  ],
  "rationale": "Performing hot work over a sewer or drain introduces a critical risk of igniting flammable gases (e.g., methane, hydrogen sulfide) that can accumulate in confined drainage systems. NFPA 51B and OSHA 1926.352 require that combustible materials and flammable atmospheres be eliminated or controlled within a 35-foot radius before hot work begins. The work must stop until the drain is isolated, the atmosphere is verified safe, and a proper hot work permit is in place.",
  "references": [
    "NFPA 51B: Standard for Fire Prevention During Welding, Cutting, and Other Hot Work",
    "OSHA 1926.352: Fire prevention"
  ]
}
